title: To Keep A True Lent.
content:
Is this a fast, to keep
        The larder lean?
            And clean
    From fat of veals and sheep?

    Is it to quit the dish
        Of flesh, yet still
            To fill
    The platter high with fish?

    Is it to fast an hour,
        Or ragg'd to go,
            Or show
    A downcast look and sour?

    No; 'tis a fast to dole
        Thy sheaf of wheat,
            And meat,
    Unto the hungry soul.

    It is to fast from strife,
        From old debate
            And hate;
    To circumcise thy life.

    To show a heart grief-rent;
        To starve thy sin,
            Not bin;
    And that's to keep thy Lent.
meaning:
This evocative piece by Robert Herrick, titled "To Keep A True Lent.", represents a masterful exploration of classic. The lines capture a profound emotional resonance...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
